title: CCX Sub-Device DFU Initiation — App Stability Check
steps: Connect a Poly CCX device (e.g., CCX 500/600) to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Device → Software Update
Verify the component list renders correctly (base station, handset, etc.).
Tap 'Start DFU' on a sub-device component (e.g., base station firmware).
Observe app behavior immediately after tapping.
expected: App remains stable and responsive. No crash, ANR, or force-close occurs. The DFU initiation dialog or progress screen appears. Actual (If Bug): App crashes immediately upon tapping 'Start DFU' on the CCX sub-device component.

title: CS540 Sub-Device DFU Initiation — App Stability Check
steps: Connect a Poly CS540 DECT headset to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Device → Software Update
Verify the component list renders correctly (headset, base station, charging stand).
Tap 'Start DFU' on the base station sub-device component.
Observe app behavior immediately after tapping.
expected: App remains stable and responsive. No crash, ANR, or force-close occurs. The DFU initiation dialog or progress screen appears. Actual (If Bug): App crashes immediately upon tapping 'Start DFU' on the CS540 base station sub-device component.

title: CCX Sub-Device DFU Initiation — Component List Rendering Before DFU
steps: Connect a Poly CCX device to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Device → Software Update
Observe the full component list rendered on screen.
Verify each sub-device entry shows firmware version and update availability.
Tap 'Start DFU' on the first sub-device component.
expected: All sub-device components render with correct firmware version strings and update status. No blank entries, 'null' values, or formatting errors. Tapping 'Start DFU' does not crash the app. Actual (If Bug): Component list shows malformed/null firmware info, or app crashes when parsing/displaying sub-device data before DFU starts.

title: CS540 Sub-Device DFU Initiation — Immediate App Backgrounding After Tap
steps: Connect a Poly CS540 DECT headset to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Device → Software Update
Tap 'Start DFU' on the headset sub-device component.
IMMEDIATELY press the Home button to background the app (within 1 second of tapping).
Wait 5 seconds, then foreground the app by tapping the app icon.
expected: App does not crash upon backgrounding or foregrounding. The DFU state is preserved or gracefully handled (either DFU continues or shows a consistent error state). No ANR or blank screen on return. Actual (If Bug): App crashes on backgrounding or shows a frozen/blank screen upon return.

title: CCX Sub-Device DFU Initiation — App Force-Kill Mid-Initiation
steps: Connect a Poly CCX device to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Device → Software Update
Tap 'Start DFU' on a sub-device component.
IMMEDIATELY (within 1 second) force-kill the app via Settings → Apps → Poly Studio Mobile → Force Stop.
Reopen the app and reconnect to the CCX device.
Navigate to: Device → Software Update
expected: App reopens without crash. The Software Update tab loads correctly showing all sub-device components with their current firmware info. No persistent crash loop. Actual (If Bug): App enters a crash loop on restart, or the Software Update tab fails to render sub-device components after the interrupted DFU initiation.

title: Device Asymmetry — Voyager Free 60+ DFU vs CCX DFU Initiation Comparison
steps: Connect a Poly Voyager Free 60+ to the app.
Navigate to: Device → Software Update
Tap 'Start DFU' on the earbuds component — verify success.
Disconnect Voyager Free 60+ and connect a Poly CCX device.
Navigate to: Device → Software Update
Tap 'Start DFU' on a CCX sub-device component.
expected: Voyager Free 60+ DFU initiation proceeds normally (dialog/progress appears). CCX DFU initiation also proceeds without crash. Both device types handle the 'Start DFU' tap identically from a stability perspective. Actual (If Bug): CCX DFU initiation crashes while Voyager Free 60+ DFU works fine, indicating device asymmetry in DFU initiation handling.

title: Device Asymmetry — CS540 Sub-Device DFU vs Earbud DFU Initiation
steps: Connect a Poly Voyager Free 60+ to the app.
Navigate to: Device → Software Update
Tap 'Start DFU' on the charge case component — verify success.
Disconnect Voyager Free 60+ and connect a Poly CS540 DECT headset.
Navigate to: Device → Software Update
Tap 'Start DFU' on the CS540 base station sub-device component.
expected: Voyager Free 60+ charge case DFU initiation proceeds normally. CS540 base station DFU initiation also proceeds without crash. Actual (If Bug): CS540 base station DFU initiation crashes while Voyager Free 60+ charge case DFU works fine, confirming device asymmetry specific to DECT/desk phone sub-device DFU.

title: CCX Sub-Device DFU — Rapid Repeated Initiation Taps
steps: Connect a Poly CCX device to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Device → Software Update
Rapidly tap 'Start DFU' on a sub-device component 10 times in quick succession (< 3 seconds).
Observe app behavior.
expected: App does not crash, ANR, or become unresponsive. Only one DFU initiation dialog/progress is triggered (duplicate taps are debounced). Actual (If Bug): App crashes due to race condition from multiple simultaneous DFU initiation requests, or multiple overlapping DFU dialogs appear.

title: CS540 Sub-Device DFU — Component List Scroll + Initiation
steps: Connect a Poly CS540 DECT headset to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Device → Software Update
Scroll the component list up and down multiple times to ensure all sub-device entries are rendered.
Tap 'Start DFU' on the last visible sub-device component (e.g., charging stand).
Observe app behavior.
expected: All sub-device components render correctly during scrolling. No blank entries, overlapping text, or rendering glitches. Tapping 'Start DFU' on any scrolled-to component does not crash the app. Actual (If Bug): App crashes when tapping DFU on a component that was scrolled into view, indicating lazy-loading or view recycling issues.

title: CCX Sub-Device DFU — Phone Rotation During Initiation
steps: Connect a Poly CCX device to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Navigate to: Device → Software Update
Tap 'Start DFU' on a sub-device component.
Immediately rotate the phone to landscape orientation.
Rotate back to portrait orientation.
Observe app behavior.
expected: App does not crash during or after rotation. The DFU initiation state is preserved across configuration changes. No blank screen, overlapping UI elements, or ANR. Actual (If Bug): App crashes on rotation due to improper handling of DFU initiation state during Activity recreation.
